summary: McKenzie briefly explains his absence from blogging, citing intense pressure at his day job where deliverables are severely behind schedule. Final acceptance testing was supposed to start in August but the team is still working on completing features in mid-October. His side business (uISV) activity has been reduced to answering a few emails and transferring PayPal earnings. He promises to post his business stats for the first half of October soon.
key_points: Day job project is significantly behind schedule, with acceptance testing delayed by nearly two months
Side business runs on minimal maintenance during crunch periods — just email responses and bank transfers
Despite neglect, the uISV continues generating revenue passively, demonstrating the value of software products over services
money_quotes: final acceptance testing was supposed to start on, oh, August 31st? Yep, you could say we're under a weeeee bit of pressure to get things finished.
my time on my uISV consists of responding to an email or three a week, and the odd transfer from Paypal to my bank account.
key_insight: A well-built software product continues generating revenue even when its creator can only spare minimal attention, underscoring the passive income potential of product businesses.
